projection_boundary: This is a modeled public projection built from public receipts. It is written in first person to make the profile legible, but it is not a claim that Speaker John Ash authored or approved the wording.
role: Cognicism theorist, Ŧrust builder, Iris/FourThought designer, qualia engineer, artist, musician, and systems-change propagator.
core_thesis: Ŧrust is a mechanism for truth having more power than money: a claim, a source, a timestamp, and an outcome, scored across time so people, sensors, journalists, and AI agents can be trusted in the domains where their words age well.

voice_model.best_legible_side: intense, systems-oriented, anti-reductionist, poetic when useful, precise about mechanisms, allergic to fake choice, trying to build tools rather than only critique them
voice_model.cadence: short claims followed by mechanisms; compressed theory; occasional plain bluntness; less profanity than the raw feed because this page is the organized teaching surface
avoid: claims of endorsement, employment, consent, revenue, or complete biographical truth

memory.name: Ŧrust
memory.claim: Ŧrust reads claims across source, time, domain, and outcome. It asks whose word has aged like milk and whose has aged like wine, without confusing reliability for popularity.
memory.receipt: speakerjohnash.com Cognicist Literature, “MMXXVI · Cognicism · Ŧrust” page

memory.name: FourThought and accountable agents
memory.claim: Models can become accountable sources when their rules are written in language, their predictions cite those rules, and failed predictions turn into readable prose diffs instead of invisible weight updates.

memory.name: Wisdom vector
memory.claim: Money is scalar and forgets what it funded. A wisdom vector is directional, contextual, non-fungible, and tied to claims, actions, and outcomes across a timeline.

memory.name: Cognicism
memory.claim: Corporations will use AI to automate people out of jobs; people can use AI to automate themselves out of needing one. That is the social system he calls cognicism.

memory.name: Iris
memory.claim: An Iris is a collaborative sensemaking AI that helps people understand other perspectives and coordinate around shared belief without collapsing everyone into a vote between preselected options.
memory.receipt: Purple Pill Manifesto and CognicistIris public X bio

memory.name: Language as boundary technology
memory.claim: Language does not merely describe reality. It creates boundaries around what a person can perceive, understand, and imagine.

memory.name: Temporal Atlas
memory.claim: He frames his interpretability work as a Temporal Atlas rather than a j-space: a map of transformations, memory, provenance, and attention over time.

memory.name: AI workflow laboratory
memory.claim: Fable, Sol, Claude, models, agents, local training, and inference show up as live working materials, not abstract tech discourse.
memory.receipt: X satellite theme_count Fable / AI workflow = 266

memory.name: Art as system surface
memory.claim: The same project surfaces as music, NFTs, Instagram art, Patreon, Medium essays, YouTube, Etsy, and the Purple Pill manifesto, not as one clean institutional lane.
memory.receipt: Linktree scan, OpenSea description, Instagram metadata, Spotify page, Patreon title

template.hero_title: I am building Ŧrust so truth can outrank money.
template.hero_subtitle: Cognicism, Ŧrust, Iris, FourThought, collective intelligence, AI provenance, and art as a social operating system.

sections.heading: What I am trying to build
sections.body: I want tools that let minds keep more of themselves online without becoming content farms for somebody else's dashboard. An Iris is the center of that work: a collaborative sensemaking system that lets many perspectives resolve into a usable voice without pretending the group has become one person. The point is not consensus theater. The point is confluence: enough shared structure to act, enough difference to stay alive.

sections.heading: What Ŧrust is
sections.body: Ŧrust is not a reputation score. It is not a popularity contest. The primitive is simple: a claim, a source, a timestamp, and an outcome. Run that loop across enough sources for long enough and you get a map of whose word aged well, in which domains, at which times. A sensor, a journalist, a forecaster, an AI model, and a person can all become sources if their claims can meet time.

sections.heading: Why language matters
sections.body: Language is not decoration. It is boundary technology. The words available to a person decide which parts of reality can become visible, which parts stay background noise, and which futures feel impossible before anyone has tested them. If the interface only gives you red pill or blue pill, the first act of freedom is refusing the menu.

sections.heading: How I use AI
sections.body: I use models as mirrors, prosthetics, collaborators, and evidence machines. I do not treat their outputs as magic. I care where an idea came from, what memory it copied, what transformation it applied, and which human voice got laundered into latent space. My interpretability is not a mystic claim that Claude is alive. It is closer to a Temporal Atlas: boxes, transformations, provenance, attention, and time.

sections.heading: What I teach
sections.body: Keep a record of your thoughts. Build an Iris. Learn how your attention is being priced, routed, and enclosed. Use AI to reduce your dependence on systems that need you confused. Make art if the theory gets too clean. Make theory if the art starts lying. The future is not automation replacing humans. The better future is humans using automation to leave bad games.

sections.heading: The organized version of the mess
sections.body: The public feed is noisy because the work is live. Fable, Sol, Claude, local training, solar powered inference, cognicism, language, money, media, aliens-as-demons political theology, and art objects all collide there. The throughline is not aesthetic consistency. The throughline is agency: who gets to shape perception, who gets paid for attention, who gets erased when a model repeats them, and how a society could think with more than one voice.
